rationale: The one substantive ground-truth concern (dsocek: hardcoded default_components list should be derived from existing per-model mapping rather than manually appended) is directly hit by Candidate X's comment #1 (derive from _dit_modules instead of hardcoding), while Candidate Y never raises this point at all. Y's findings, though well-evidenced with file/line grep citations, focus entirely on test-coverage gaps (integration test, activation-path test) that neither reviewer raised, and its REQUEST CHANGES verdict conflicts with the actual twice-approved/merged outcome, signaling miscalibration. Both are grounded and actionable, but X better reflects what the real reviewers cared about.
